name: obsidian-markdown
description: Create and edit Obsidian Flavored Markdown with wikilinks, embeds, callouts, properties, and other Obsidian-specific syntax. Use when working with .md files in Obsidian, or when the user mentions wikilinks, callouts, frontmatter, tags, embeds, or Obsidian notes.
capabilities: Create valid wikilinks with aliases and heading references
Format callouts with all supported types and foldable options
Add frontmatter properties with correct YAML syntax
Embed notes, images, audio, PDFs, and code blocks
Use block IDs for precise internal linking
Format task lists, tables, and math equations
author: ZhanlinCui
securitySummary: All static security findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation/reference guide for Obsidian Markdown syntax. The 'external_commands' alerts are markdown backtick syntax being misidentified as shell commands. The 'network' findings are legitimate documentation URLs (commonmark.org, help.obsidian.md) used as references. The 'weak_crypto' and 'system_reconnaissance' alerts are YAML frontmatter and Obsidian block IDs being misinterpreted.
valueStatement: This skill enables AI agents to create and edit Obsidian Flavored Markdown with proper syntax for wikilinks, callouts, embeds, properties, and other Obsidian-specific features.
limitations: Does not execute Obsidian plugins or JavaScript
Does not interact with Obsidian API or vault files
Does not render or preview markdown visually
Does not manage Obsidian settings or configurations

useCases.targetUser: Users building second brain or PKM systems
useCases.title: Generate Knowledge Base Notes
useCases.description: Create interconnected notes with wikilinks for building a personal knowledge management system

useCases.title: Format Technical Documentation
useCases.description: Write documentation with code blocks, callouts, and embedded files using proper syntax

useCases.title: Create Project Tracking Notes
useCases.description: Set up project notes with properties, task lists, and status tracking

promptTemplates.title: Basic Note Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an Obsidian note with title 'Meeting Notes', add frontmatter with date and tags, include a callout for action items, and link to other notes using wikilinks.

promptTemplates.title: Link to Specific Section
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a note that links to a specific heading in another note using the wikilink syntax. Include an embed of a code block from a third note.
promptTemplates.scenario: Linking to headings and embedding content

promptTemplates.title: Complex Property Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a note with multiple property types: text, number, checkbox, date, list of tags, and related links using Obsidian properties syntax.

promptTemplates.title: Callout with Foldable Content
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a note with a foldable callout that shows a warning, includes nested content, and collapses by default.

bestPractices: Use consistent naming conventions for wikilinks to maintain vault organization
Add frontmatter properties to enable Obsidian queries and organization features
Use descriptive aliases in wikilinks for readability in published notes
Use appropriate callout types to communicate information hierarchy
antiPatterns: Using standard Markdown links instead of wikilinks when linking to vault notes
Forgetting to URL-encode spaces in Markdown-style links
Using incorrect YAML syntax in frontmatter properties
Not using block IDs when needing precise links to specific content

faq.question: What is Obsidian Flavored Markdown?
faq.answer: Obsidian Flavored Markdown extends standard Markdown with features like wikilinks, callouts, embeds, and frontmatter properties for note metadata.

faq.question: What is the difference between wikilinks and Markdown links?
faq.answer: Wikilinks use double brackets [[Note Name]] and automatically create links to notes. Markdown links use [text](url) syntax and require full file paths or URLs.

faq.question: How do I link to a specific heading in another note?
faq.answer: Use wikilink syntax with # symbol: [[Note Name#Heading]]. The heading must exist in the target note.

faq.question: What are Obsidian properties?
faq.answer: Properties are YAML key-value pairs at the top of a note (frontmatter) that add metadata like tags, dates, aliases, and custom fields.

faq.question: Can I embed entire notes or just parts?
faq.answer: You can embed full notes with ![[Note Name]], specific headings with ![[Note Name#Heading]], or specific blocks with ![[Note Name#^block-id]].

faq.question: What callout types does Obsidian support?
faq.answer: Obsidian supports: note, abstract, info, todo, tip, success, question, warning, failure, danger, bug, example, and quote.
